hypothesis: If CHROMA uses larger frozen Llama encoders (1B→3B→8B) and deeper trainable decoders (2→4→8 layers), then it will produce multilayer thin-film designs with lower CIE76 ΔE to target colors and better generalization to held-out optical targets and paraphrased prompts across viewing angles and layer counts, because greater encoder and decoder capacity improves the mapping from natural language to material-thickness sequences.
